mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regressions, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of joint occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary easing reflects improved capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during easing periods, with Rank IC falling below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with rising corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sudden contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances following a greater than 5% housing starts decline, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive real policy conditions, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with accuracy below 45%, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in predictive models over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% during periods of stable unemployment confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 2.5% alongside stable unemployment, or the regime filter eliminates all predictive power.
